channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Steam Controller Release Date Potentially Leaked
item.content:encoded:
A Japanese website has seemingly provided the release date for the Steam controller along with a bunch of images.

The upcoming Steam Controller has been in the news a lot recently. First, we saw Valve secretly add an unboxing video for the upcoming controller, and later, we saw the price of this device drop thanks to an early review. Now, the release date and images have also been leaked.

Steam Controller Release Date Leaks With Comparison Images

According to information posted on the Japanese publication 4Gamer’s website, the new Steam Controller is set to release on May 4, 2026; however, that article has since been removed. Moreover, the new images (that players can check out here) reveal how the controller compares in size to the DualSense and Xbox Wireless controllers, and let me tell you, it is considerably bigger. It also reveals weight, overall features, buttons, and design.

For those unaware, the Steam Controller 2 was officially revealed back in November 2025, when we got to see standout features such as new trackpads, four grip buttons, and gyro motion. It also features a puck that acts as a wireless transmitter for the device, as well as a charger when you aren’t playing. Interested readers can check out the full specification sheet detailing all the new features for the Steam Controller 2 here.

In other news, a dataminer has found mentions of SteamGPT, which could help with platform support and CS2 anti-cheat. Also, Steam is developing a feature that estimates the framerate a game will run at on a player’s system. What are your thoughts on the Steam Controller’s release on May 4? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Assassin’s Creed 1 potentially being the next remake. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Assassin’s Creed 1 Could Be the Next Remake, It’s Claimed
item.content:encoded:
According to a new claim, Ubisoft could be working on a remake of the original Assassin’s Creed game.

Ubisoft has begun exploring remakes, with Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced as the first title in this series. Players are now speculating about what might come next. While several games in the franchise are strong contenders, an insider claims to have received information about the next Assassin’s Creed remake.

Assassin’s Creed 1 Remake Might Be On the Way

The Assassin’s Creed YouTube channel and streamer xj0nathan has claimed on X (formerly Twitter) that they heard from a contact in 2023 that “AC1” (Assassin’s Creed 1) could be the next remake. Yves Guillemot confirmed in 2024 that several remakes were in the works, allowing them to modernize and explore the worlds of older Assassin’s Creed titles; however, we weren’t sure which games were being remade.

In the latest episode of Insider Gaming Weekly, it was mentioned that another Assassin’s Creed Remake was in the works, and it was one of the “earlier” games. However, we haven’t been able to confirm which game it is yet.

Talking about remakes, practically every detail about the Assassin’s Creed Black Flag remake had leaked before the official reveal, so there’s a chance that we might know which remake is next once several leaks start popping up. However, Ubisoft has yet to officially confirm which Assassin’s Creed game they plan to remagine next. As we wait for its arrival, check out the new features in Resynced, including reworked combat and gameplay elements.

About Assassin’s Creed 1

In the original Assassin’s Creed title, players get to play as franchise favorite character, Altair, as they explore the historical Holy Land of the 12th century. You also get to play as another favorite character, Desmond Miles, in the present day as he investigates the Animus in Abstergo Industries. The game’s core mechanics, such as eavesdropping, interrogation, pickpocketing, parkour, climbing, stealth, and more, all started from this game.

In other news, here are the PC system requirements for Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced. Additionally, the upcoming AC remake will not feature DLC or multiplayer. What are your thoughts on the original Assassin’s Creed game receiving a remake? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Atari acquiring a studio focused on PS1 emulation. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: All BLAST R6 Major Salt Lake City 2026 Teams Confirmed
item.content:encoded:
The teams for BLAST Rainbow 6 Siege Major Salt Lake City 2026 are set after the conclusion of the final regional splits, confirming all 16 teams, and here they will start in the competition.

The final spots were confirmed on the weekend following the conclusion of the Kickoff splits in the Pacific region and in China.

All BLAST R6 Major Salt Lake City 2026 Teams

The top seeds from EML, NAL, SAL, APL Asia, and the CNL will begin the competition in the Phase 2 Swiss Stage, with lower-ranked teams and small-region qualifiers starting in Phase 1’s GSL format, where four of the eight teams will qualify for Phase 2.

Phase 2:

  • G2
  • Virtus.pro
  • Twisted Minds
  • Five Fears
  • Wildcard
  • DarkZero
  • Furia
  • Ninjas in Pyjamas
  • FaZe Clan
  • CAG
  • Weibo Gaming
  • All Gamers

Phase 1:

  • Daystar
  • EDward Gaming
  • Enterprise
  • Four Angry Men
  • LOS
  • Shopify Rebellion
  • Team Falcons
  • Wolves

How each team qualified for the BLAST R6 Salt Lake City Major

Europe MENA League Kickoff saw a significant shakeup, with G2 moving from third in Stage 2 2025 to claiming the 2025 Kickoff title. Virtus.pro finished runners-up, after a clean Upper Bracket run was ended by an imperious G2 roster.

For Falcons, it was the opposite trajectory as G2, falling from winners to barely scraping into the event as the fourth seed, having to start in Phase 1 after losing the Lower Bracket Final to regional rivals, Twisted Minds.

NAL saw M80’s hegemony in the region collapse, with Five Fears now the top dog after defeating Wildcard in the Upper Bracket Final. Dark Zeo defeated Shopify Rebellion to take the final Phase 2 position, with Spacestation Gaming also missing out on Salt Lake City.

South America saw Six Invitational 2026 winners FaZe Clan only qualify third after Fluxo W7M upset them in the Upper Bracket, only to miss out on qualifying themselves.

FURIA once again showed their class with victory in the Kickoff split, with NIP claiming the second spot and LOS taking the Phase 2 spot in an impressive underdog run.

Weibo Gaming secured the Phase 2 APL Asia spot, defeating Daystar, who will start in Phase 2. Joining them in Phase 2 are small region winners, CAG from Asia North and Enterprise from Oceania.

CN League saw All Gamers dominate, only dropping a single map on their way to securing the top Chinese spot, with EDward Gaming, Wolves, and Four Angry Men all set to start in Phase 2.

Wolves will play the event in a severely depleted state after the sudden departures of Recap and Ip ‘KarTy’ Chun. The pair were released for ‘personal reasons’ (translated) according to Wolves’ Weibo, but rumours surrounding the release have pointed to historic social media posts as a potential reason.

Who do you think will win the R6 Salt Lake City Major? Let us know on Insider Gaming’s Discord!
For more news, see KeSPA’s reported withdrawal from the Esports Nations Cup.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson
